item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: The proposed changes look good to me!

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright. Two references here can be made into easy in-line links that will add a little more wikiwalk depth to the article. Maybe. The first one is turning the Serpent's Hand reference into a link to their hub. Additionally, the character of Riven Mercer that is mentioned in this skip is a recurring figure in Zyn's Kiryu Labs tales, and a link to those tales would not be too far out of line, I feel. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Addendum 2175-1: On ██/██/████, seven SCP-2175-1 instances were recovered, each containing a unique verse variation on the lyrics of the ballad “Scarborough Fair”. These recoveries coincided with the appearance of a group of seven individuals on the bridge, one of whom was under Foundation investigation for potential connections to the Serpent’s Hand. Though Foundation personnel were able to recover the documents, none of the seven individuals could be located after their initial sighting. The circumstances of their disappearance are currently unknown.

To this:

Addendum 2175-1: On ██/██/████, seven SCP-2175-1 instances were recovered, each containing a unique verse variation on the lyrics of the ballad “Scarborough Fair”. These recoveries coincided with the appearance of a group of seven individuals on the bridge, one of whom was under Foundation investigation for potential connections to the Serpent’s Hand. Though Foundation personnel were able to recover the documents, none of the seven individuals could be located after their initial sighting. The circumstances of their disappearance are currently unknown.

Changing this:

Addendum 2175-2: Analysis of the verse variations deemed the lyrics to be non-anomalous. The Scarborough Protocol was created on ██/██/████ by Doctor R. Mercer to address the potential of SCP-2175 manifesting documents consisting of sensitive information when Foundation agents come into contact with the location. Repeated trials of the behavioral procedure (mentally reciting the recovered verses and focusing only tangentially on the bridge) have demonstrated fairly consistent success: roughly 70% of manifested documents that do result from Foundation proxy activity consist entirely of lyrics from the ballad, with no other information present.

To this:

Addendum 2175-2: Analysis of the verse variations deemed the lyrics to be non-anomalous. The Scarborough Protocol was created on ██/██/████ by Doctor R. Mercer to address the potential of SCP-2175 manifesting documents consisting of sensitive information when Foundation agents come into contact with the location. Repeated trials of the behavioral procedure (mentally reciting the recovered verses and focusing only tangentially on the bridge) have demonstrated fairly consistent success: roughly 70% of manifested documents that do result from Foundation proxy activity consist entirely of lyrics from the ballad, with no other information present.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
were they written out sprayed in the bridge? I think that little detail is missing.

Nope, just scrawled on pieces of paper like the rest of the stuff recovered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this scp concept. But one question comes to my mind when we talk about Serpent Hand's diagrams, were they written out sprayed in the bridge? I think that little detail is missing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it even when all I knew about the effect was "magical post-it note, probably helps you keep from having to cross the bridge more often than necessary." The GoI reference was a plus, and the last note was an excellent stinger. (I generally enjoy SCPs that hint at having more mystery to them than the Foundation has yet investigated- not just "why/how is this happening?" but "is there something else going on here too?", for instance.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, I didn't see this for some time, it looks like. I've changed the phrasing to the second example you've given. Thanks, Ralphomon!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's good.

One thing, though: "Windsor Bridge of Berkshire county in England" reads strangely to a native Brit. Maybe "Windsor Bridge, in the county of Berkshire, UK" or "Windsor Bridge, connecting the towns of Windsor and Eton in Berkshire, UK" would be better? The phrasing "[county name] county" isn't usually used in British English. Unless you're going for an Americanised voice here, of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Give it enough time, I'm sure someone will come up with a floopty-doopty Waypoint locator for the Foundation to use. :|

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you posted it Zyn.
This version is much better than what I saw in chat and is what I would consider "the bee's knees."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes. But the Foundation doesn't know that. ;)

Fixed! The documents just appear on the walkway, pretty much. Unaware people will step on them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. Is it a Way point or something? My only other thought is, where do the documents manifest? Just on the ground at random? In the air? Attached to the bridge? In the water?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Despite my disappointment in it not being a renaissance faire based SCP, I quite enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Admins, when are we getting comment upvotes

How much dogecoin do I have to send in

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An amphiptere flew through town and looked through fruit stalls with a frown
He was so tired of stealing loot that he wanted to eat some fruit
The amphiptere then ate an apple, then he felt the urge to grapple
Then he ate a pair of pears, and tried to climb up some tall stairs
The amphiptere then ate a guava, then he spewed out flaming lava
Then he ate a big strawberry, and made faces that were scary
The amphiptere then ate an orange, but then he crashed into a door hinge
[…]
Then he ate a peach for fun, and decided he was done.
The amphiptere flew out of town, with his frown turned upside-down.

Edit: Fruit dragon is now a thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it when I first read it and it's improved in several ways since. +1.

If anyone's wondering, an amphiptere is a winged serpent that appears in 19th century heraldry, similar to a lindworm except that it's more commonly legless.

item.title: Author Notes
item.content:encoded:
Remember me to one who lives there…

Listen to Scarborough Fair. And then listen to Over the Hills and Far Away because it’s awesome and also I listened to that while writing?

Many thanks to the people who read this in chat (and anyone I might have missed!): Conwell (more SH!), Gottagofeast (storyline), TwistedGears (less sads), DisquietRiot (less over-subtle), DrMann (proxies), Vince, LordDeath, Accelerando (SH development), lurkd , thedeadlymoose (SH!!!), Silber (he named this), rumetzen, Trar from wandererslibrary (for the fairy hunting), Wogglebug (inconsistency fixes), Eskobear (phrasing), Clef

I did quite a bit of Wikipedia research for this article. >.>

Why 2175? I looked up some Irish mythology. Someone can call me on this if I’m wrong, of course.
21 = 3 x 7 (two lucky numbers)
17 is said to be mystical.
5 is significant and found in many designs.

The picture is from the Windsor Bridge Wikipedia article, and licensed under Wikimedia Commons.
